_meta.description: Vertical explorer — all 11 commodity chains with full step-by-step detail, flags, and cross-chain links.

oil.note: Refining (crack spreads), offshore drilling (day-rate cycle), and tankers are oil-specific layers. Tidewater (TDW) is the overlooked OSV picks-and-shovels play. Petrochemicals link oil to defense and aerospace composites. AI data centers are the new structural gas demand driver.

chain.r: Royalty income on production; no capex exposure
chain.d: Viper Energy (VNOM — Permian, Diamondback drop-down growth structure; only growth-oriented mineral rights vehicle); Black Stone Minerals (BSM — multi-basin income); PHX Minerals. Most royalty trusts are terminal-decline income vehicles — VNOM is the exception with a structural drop-down model.

chain.r: 2D/3D seismic surveys; frontier basin appraisal
chain.d: TGS (Oslo — data library model, ~80% gross margins, recurring licensing revenue; most defensible seismic asset); PGS (Oslo — seismic vessels, more cyclical). Active frontiers: Namibia Orange Basin (TotalEnergies, Shell — potentially largest deepwater discovery since Guyana), Guyana Stabroek (ExxonMobil — 11B+ barrels).

chain.r: Appraisal, FEED studies, FPSO procurement, government agreements
chain.d: FPSOs: SBM Offshore (SBMO — dominant global FPSO owner/operator), Modec (Japan), BW Offshore (BWOE). Tidewater (TDW — best-in-class OSV fleet; modern diversified vessels; multi-year high-rate charter backlog; routinely overlooked in oil picks-and-shovels discussion).

chain.r: UDW floaters, jackups, shale E&P, oilfield services
chain.d: Shale: DVN, FANG, COP, MRO, OVV. UDW: RIG, VAL, NE, DO — utilization ~90%+. OFS: SLB (largest, international), HAL (US fracking ~40% market share), BKR. OSVs: Tidewater (TDW) — the overlooked picks-and-shovels play for deepwater.

chain.r: Crude gathering from wellheads; water/gas separation; first compression
chain.d: Plains All American (PAA — largest US crude pipeline MLP; Permian + Texas); Crestwood; Sunoco LP. MVCs (minimum volume commitments) provide cash flow floor for gathering MLPs regardless of market conditions.

chain.r: Crude → fuels; naphtha/ethane → ethylene/polyolefins
chain.d: VLO (largest independent US refiner; major coking capacity — structurally advantaged); MPC; PSX; PBF; HF Sinclair (DINO — lube specialist). Post-2020 global refinery closures structurally elevated crack spreads. Petrochems: LYB (world's largest PP producer), DOW (polyethylene), WLK (PVC).

chain.r: Crude trunk lines; VLCC crude tankers; MR/LR product tankers
chain.d: PAA (pipelines). VLCC: FRO, DHT, INSW. Product tankers: STNG. Russian crude rerouting post-2022 added structural tonne-miles (longer routes to India/China) — fleet average age >18yr limits new supply response.

chain.r: Crude tankage, SPR, VLCC-capable export terminals
chain.d: Vopak (Netherlands — global chokepoint tank terminals at strategic waterways). Moda Midstream (Ingleside TX — only fully VLCC-capable US crude export terminal). Enterprise EPD (Houston Ship Channel).

chain.r: LDCs, gas-fired power, fuel retail, industrial feedstocks
chain.d: LDCs: ATO, SWX, OGS. Gas power: VST (ERCOT dominant, largest competitive power company), NRG, Calpine (private). AI data centers: gas-fired CCGT is the default 24/7 dispatchable power solution — this is the structural demand link from the oil/gas chain to the AI infrastructure buildout.

chain.r: Lube re-refining; plastic recycling; RNG from landfills
chain.d: CLH (Safety-Kleen — largest NA lube re-refiner); HCCI. RNG: CLNE (distribution), MNTK (landfill gas capture). LYB (molecular plastic recycling), EMN (carbon renewal technology).

ng.note: Your existing thesis. Haynesville proximity to Gulf LNG terminals is the strategic sub-basin advantage. Three structural demand drivers: European energy security (locked into 20yr SPAs), Asian LNG growth, AI data center power demand. US LNG export chain is now a NATO strategic foreign policy asset that cannot be unwound.

chain.r: Gas royalty income; mineral rights trusts; non-operated interests
chain.d: BSM (multi-basin, income-oriented); PHX Minerals (Midcontinent); VNOM (Permian). NRIs in Haynesville Basin = cleanest LNG supply chain royalty exposure — geographic proximity to Gulf export terminals.

chain.r: Basin characterization; seismic; reserve booking in major gas plays
chain.d: EQT (largest US gas producer — Appalachian); AR (strong NGL mix); SWN/Expand Energy (Haynesville — most strategically positioned for LNG export proximity); RRC; CNX.

chain.r: Leasehold, pad development planning, LNG project FID
chain.d: LNG FID requires 20yr SPAs with creditworthy counterparties. Economics: Henry Hub strip + tolling fee (~$2.50-3.50/MMBTU). Venture Global CP2 (20 MTPA) is the largest US LNG project in the development pipeline.

chain.r: Horizontal drilling, hydraulic fracturing, wellhead production
chain.d: EQT, AR, SWN, RRC for Appalachian/Haynesville. HP (AutoSlide smart rig technology — market leader in efficiency), PTEN. Fracking: SLB, HAL, LBRT (Liberty Energy). Lateral lengths now routinely exceeding 15,000-20,000ft.

chain.r: Wellhead collection; compression at regular intervals
chain.d: WES (Oxy-affiliated); HESM (Bakken dedicated); Summit Midstream; Crestwood. Archrock (AROC — largest US contract compression operator; Permian-heavy; benefits structurally as reservoir pressure declines requiring more compression over time).

chain.r: Cryogenic plants remove ethane/propane/butane; H2S/CO2 treating
chain.d: WMB (Transco backbone + largest US gas processing); TRGP (Permian G&P powerhouse, best-in-class NGL fractionation); OKE (post-Magellan merger). NGL ethane recovery/rejection decisions swing with ethylene cracker demand — cross-vertical link to petrochemicals.

chain.r: Cooling to -162°C; multi-train facilities; 20yr SPAs — NATO strategic asset
chain.d: Cheniere (LNG — Sabine Pass 6 trains + Corpus Christi + Stage 3 FID; world's #1 LNG exporter); Venture Global (VGAS — Calcasieu Pass + CP2 in development); NFE; Sempra. US is now world's #1 LNG exporter. European energy security locked into US/Qatar LNG for 20+ years via 20yr SPAs.

chain.r: Cryogenic carriers; spot + long-term charter markets
chain.d: Flex LNG (FLNG — high-spec vessels, excellent charter coverage locked at 2022 energy crisis highs); Golar LNG (GLNG — also FLNG/floating production assets); MISC Berhad (Petronas-affiliated).

chain.r: FSRU/onshore regas; LDCs; gas-fired power + AI data centers
chain.d: Import terminals: EE (FSRU specialist), Höegh LNG, NFE. European buildout post-2022. LDCs: ATO, SWX, OGS. Gas power for AI: VST, NRG, Calpine — gas-fired CCGT is the default solution for always-on data center power demand.

chain.r: Renewable natural gas; landfill gas capture; flare recovery
chain.d: CLNE (RNG distribution, transport fleet fueling); MNTK (landfill gas capture); Archaea Energy (BP-acquired). Permian flare capture is both an ESG priority and recovers economically valuable gas.

nu.note: Two sub-verticals: uranium supply chain (5 discrete processing steps — each an oligopoly, each a potential chokepoint) and nuclear power generation. Enrichment is the most acute strategic vulnerability in the US energy supply chain. Nuclear = preferred 24/7 clean AI data center baseload. CEG-Microsoft TMI is the commercial template.

chain.r: Physical U3O8 trusts; investment vehicles and structural demand floors
chain.d: Sprott Physical Uranium Trust (SPUT/U.UN — ~50M lbs U3O8 held; functions as demand floor via discretionary buying when spot < NAV; ~1.5yr of US annual demand stockpiled); Yellow Cake plc (YCA — London-listed). Physical trusts = commodity price leverage with zero operational risk.

chain.r: Geological surveys; RC drilling targeting unconformity, ISR deposits
chain.d: NXE (Arrow, Athabasca Basin — potentially world's largest undeveloped high-grade uranium deposit); DNN (Wheeler River, ISR-amenable); FCU (Triple R); UEC (US ISR: Texas, Wyoming, South Dakota); Boss Energy (Honeymoon, Australia — ISR, restarted 2023).

chain.r: Feasibility, EA, permitting; SMR developer activity
chain.d: US ISR projects: shorter permitting (2-4yr) vs. underground mines (5-10yr). BWX Technologies (BWXT) — leading US naval reactor manufacturer + BWRX-300 SMR component supplier. DoD + DOE funding for domestic supply chain rebuilding programs.

chain.r: Conventional open pit/underground or in-situ recovery (ISR)
chain.d: CCO (McArthur River/Key Lake; world's highest-grade; ~15% global supply); KAP (~40% global ISR, Kazakhstan; geopolitically ambiguous); Paladin (Langer Heinrich, Namibia — restarted 2023); UEC (US ISR); Boss Energy. Top 3 = ~65% of global supply.

chain.r: Milling ore to U3O8 concentrate; physical trusts buy directly from mills
chain.d: Cameco Key Lake mill (world's largest licensed uranium mill). Physical trusts (SPUT, YCA) buy direct from millers — trust buying has been a significant structural factor in spot price recovery since 2021.

chain.r: Converting U3O8 to uranium hexafluoride; only ~4 commercial facilities globally
chain.d: Cameco Fuel Manufacturing (Port Hope, Canada); Orano (Malvési, France); ConverDyn (Honeywell Metropolis, Illinois — restarted 2023 after years of Russian dependency). Very few global facilities = critical single point of failure risk.

chain.r: Centrifuge enrichment raises U-235 from 0.7% to 3-5% for LWR fuel
chain.d: MOST ACUTE STRATEGIC VULNERABILITY in US energy supply chain. Urenco (private, UK/NL/DE, UUSA plant New Mexico); Centrus Energy (LEU — ONLY US company with active domestic enrichment; HALEU program at Piketon OH). TENEX/Rosatom was ~28% of US SWU before 2024 import ban. Year 1-2 of a decade-long rebuild.

chain.r: Enriched UF6 → UO2 ceramic pellets → zircaloy fuel rod assemblies
chain.d: Westinghouse (Brookfield-owned; Columbia SC + Springfields UK); Framatome (EDF subsidiary, France); GNF (GE-Hitachi + Toshiba JV, Wilmington NC). 5-7yr reactor qualification cycles = extremely sticky customer relationships and recurring revenue — once qualified, utilities rarely switch.

chain.r: Engineering/constructing nuclear plants; modular factory-built SMR designs
chain.d: GE-Hitachi BWRX-300 (300 MWe; Ontario Power 4-unit order — first Western commercial SMR contract ever); X-energy Xe-100 (Dow Chemical site); TerraPower Natrium; BWX Technologies (BWXT — naval reactor + SMR component manufacturer, the key enabling company for US SMR buildout).

chain.r: Running reactor fleets; 24/7 baseload electricity; AI data center PPAs
chain.d: Constellation Energy (CEG — largest US nuclear fleet ~10% US electricity; Microsoft Three Mile Island restart (2024) = 20yr PPA template at ~$110/MWh for 24/7 carbon-free power; pipeline of similar deals). Duke, Dominion, Southern Company (Vogtle 3+4 completed).

chain.r: Interim spent fuel storage; reprocessing; MOX fuel manufacture
chain.d: Holtec (private — dry cask storage + HOLTEC SMR-300); EnergySolutions (private — Clive UT disposal); Orano (La Hague, France — world's leading closed fuel cycle reprocessing operation).

cu.note: Most important critical mineral for electrification. ~8Mt/yr structural deficit forecast by 2030. Average discovery-to-production = 16-17 years. TC/RC near-zero or negative in 2024 = earliest market signal of structural supply squeeze. Every EV, transformer, grid cable, and data center requires copper.

chain.r: Metal streams at copper mines; best risk-adjusted exposure in the vertical
chain.d: FNV (multi-commodity streams at copper mines); WPM (Antamina Ag, Salobo Au, Constancia Ag — all at copper operations); RGLD; SAND. Streaming = Cu upside without capex, processing risk, or jurisdictional concentration. Sits above the Chinese smelting chokepoint entirely — geopolitically optimal structure.

chain.r: Greenfield discovery, feasibility, permitting — 15-20yr lead time in democracies
chain.d: Solaris Resources (SLS — Warintza, Ecuador); Filo Corp (BHP+Lundin JV — Filo del Sol); Regulus Resources; Surge Copper. CRITICAL: permitting in democratic countries takes 15-20yr vs. 3-5yr in authoritarian states. This permitting asymmetry is the dominant structural constraint on Western copper supply.

chain.r: Open pit porphyry (0.3-1.5% Cu); underground block caving
chain.d: SCCO (Grupo Mexico — lowest-cost major globally; Mexico + Peru); FCX (Grasberg Indonesia + Morenci AZ — US domestic production); Antofagasta (ANTO.L — pure copper Chilean specialist); Lundin Mining; Hudbay (HBM — Copper World AZ). Codelco (Chile, state-owned) = world's largest single producer.

chain.r: Crushing + flotation → 25-30% Cu concentrate at mine site
chain.d: Done at mine site — not separately investable. SX-EW (solvent extraction/electrowinning) hydromet produces LME cathode directly from oxide ore without smelting — SCCO and Codelco use this. TC/RC near-zero in 2024 = smelters competing for declining concentrate — historically the earliest squeeze signal.

chain.r: 1,250°C → blister copper → electrolytic refining → 99.999% LME cathode
chain.d: Aurubis (NDA.DE — largest European smelter; Hamburg + Bulgaria); Glencore (Altonorte Chile); FCX (Miami AZ). CRITICAL SIGNAL: TC/RCs near-zero or negative in 2024. Chinese smelters (Jiangxi Copper, Tongling) competing for shrinking concentrate supply. Historically the earliest possible market signal preceding a structural price squeeze.

chain.r: Capesize/Panamax bulk shipping of concentrate; LME cathode logistics
chain.d: SBLK, GOGL (bulk dry cargo — indirect commodity leverage via freight rates). Port infrastructure: Codelco Mejillones, BHP Escondida Puerto Coloso. LME warehouse stock levels are a key price signal — watch for accelerating drawdown.

chain.r: LME cathode → wire rod → HV power cable → transformer windings
chain.d: Nexans (NEX.PA — HV cables including subsea offshore wind; multi-year backlog); Prysmian (PRY.MI — world's largest cable company; major HV/EHV grid + subsea backlogs from European TSOs). Each GW solar ~5,000T Cu; offshore wind ~8,000T.

chain.r: Power transformers, grid cables, EV wiring harnesses, DC bus bars
chain.d: Grid components: Hitachi Energy, ABB, ETN, GEV — all with multi-year backlogs. Grid EPC: PWR, EME. Transformer bottleneck: 2-4yr lead times. AI DCs + EVs + grid hardening simultaneously. EV: ~26kg Cu/vehicle.

chain.r: Cu scrap from e-waste, demolition — ~35% of global Cu supply
chain.d: Aurubis secondary division processes Cu scrap at Hamburg — strategically important domesticated non-Chinese supply. Metallo (Belgium — e-waste Cu recovery). Scrap fraction grows as electrification infrastructure ages.

li.note: Post-2022-boom cyclical correction — Li carbonate -80% from peak. Structural bull intact: 2026-2030 EV >30% penetration + grid BESS scaling creates new supply deficit. Buy-the-dip framework. Chinese processing dominance (70%+ LiOH conversion) is the strategic vulnerability. Sub-divide: Co (DRC, Glencore), Ni (Indonesian Class 2), Mn separately.

chain.r: Brine salars (Atacama, Puna) or spodumene pegmatite deposits
chain.d: Patriot Battery Metals (PMET.TO — Corvette, Quebec; world-class hard rock deposit); Argosy Minerals (AGY.AX — Rincon salar, Argentina); Sigma Lithium (SGML — Grota do Cirilo, Brazil). 7-10yr development timelines. Atacama brine constrained by water rights and indigenous consultation requirements.

chain.r: Pumping Li-rich brine; solar evaporation ponds (12-18 month process)
chain.d: ALB (La Negra/Atacama; largest Li company globally); SQM (Atacama — lowest cost globally; ongoing water rights dispute); Allkem (absorbed into Arcadium/Rio Tinto — Olaroz, Argentina). Argentina's Lithium Triangle is the growth frontier for new projects.

chain.r: Mining LCT pegmatite; flotation → SC6% spodumene concentrate
chain.d: PLS.AX (Pilbara Minerals — Pilgangoora; world's largest hard rock Li mine by volume); IGO.AX (Greenbushes JV with Tianqi — highest-grade globally at 2.7% Li). SC6% ships primarily to Chinese converters — where the processing dependency begins.

chain.r: Direct Lithium Extraction: faster, lower water use, higher recovery
chain.d: Standard Lithium (SLI — Arkansas Smackover with Koch Strategic); EnergyX (private); Lilac Solutions (private, Gates Ventures); E3 Lithium (ETL.V — Alberta oil sands brines). Pre-commercial at scale but DoE funding flowing. Could unlock non-Atacama resources including Permian produced water brines.

chain.r: Spodumene or brine → battery-grade LiOH or Li2CO3
chain.d: ~70% of global LiOH conversion is Chinese. ALB, SQM dominate Western supply. Ganfeng + Tianqi (China) are the largest converters globally. Arcadium (Rio Tinto) + Piedmont building non-Chinese conversion. IRA domestic content = forcing function for reshoring with 5-10yr build-out lag.

chain.r: Cathode active material precursors: NMC, LFP, LMFP chemistries
chain.d: >80% of global pCAM capacity is Asian. Umicore (UMI.BR — largest non-Chinese pCAM + recycling; building Kingston Ontario with Samsung SDI); BASF (Germany — Bécancour Quebec plant with DOE support); CNGR + Huayou (China dominant).

chain.r: Li-ion cells in cylindrical, prismatic, pouch; gigafactory scale
chain.d: CATL (~37% global); BYD (fully integrated); Panasonic (NV with Tesla); Samsung SDI; LGES; SK On. NA: Tesla (Texas 4680), Ultium Cells (LG+GM), Stellantis+Samsung (Indiana), Ford+SK On (BlueOval). Northvolt (Europe) in financial distress.

chain.r: Cell-to-pack assembly; battery management systems; thermal management
chain.d: Tesla (4680 structural pack); BYD (Blade Battery). BMS: APTV, BWA. Thermal mgmt: MOD, DAN. Battery cost ~35-40% of total EV — pack assembly is becoming a core OEM strategic competency.

chain.r: EV traction packs; stationary grid battery energy storage systems
chain.d: EV: Tesla, BYD, GM Ultium, Ford, Hyundai/Kia. Grid BESS: Fluence Energy (FLNC — AES+Siemens JV, leading BESS integrator); Tesla Megapack; AES Corp (AES); NextEra (NEE). BESS demand growing independently of EV — grid services economics are self-sustaining.

chain.r: Recovering Li, Co, Ni, Mn from end-of-life EV and grid batteries
chain.d: Li-Cycle (LICY — hub+spoke hydromet; strategic Western supply chain asset); Redwood Materials (private — JB Straubel; DOE loan guarantee; major OEM partnerships). DoD + IRA funding: domesticated supply bypasses Chinese processing control. 2026-2030 is the inflection point as first mass-market EVs reach end-of-life.

re.note: Most acute single geopolitical chokepoint in the universe. China controls ~85% of REE separation. LREE (Nd, Pr — magnet volume) vs HREE (Dy, Tb — high-temp performance enhancers — essentially NO Western separation). Robotics demand multiplier NOT in consensus forecasts: 6-12 REE motors per industrial robot. China already weaponizing via processing technology export restrictions.

chain.r: REE-bearing deposits (monazite, bastnäsite); mixed LREE/HREE profiles
chain.d: MP Materials (MP — Mountain Pass CA; only significant US REE mine; LREE-heavy; ~15% global LREE supply; currently still ships ~50% output to China for separation — the dependency they are actively eliminating); Lynas (LYC.AX — Mt. Weld Australia; largest non-China miner/processor globally); UUUU (White Mesa Mill — REE from monazite sand alongside uranium).

chain.r: Ore extraction; acid cracking (monazite/bastnäsite) → mixed RE carbonate
chain.d: MP Materials (Mountain Pass processing); Lynas (Mt. Weld → Gebeng Malaysia + Seadrift TX DoD-funded facility under construction); Iluka (ILU.AX — Eneabba refinery, Australia, cracking monazite from mineral sands processing).

chain.r: Solvent extraction separates individual REEs; China controls ~85-90%
chain.d: THE strategic chokepoint of the entire critical minerals universe. Outside China: MP (NdPr at Mountain Pass + Fort Worth TX); Lynas Malaysia (NdPr + limited HREE) + Lynas Texas (DoD-funded, under construction); Solvay (La Rochelle — small HREE volumes). HREE (Dy, Tb): essentially NO Western separation capacity at all.

chain.r: Electrolytic/metallothermic reduction of separated oxides → rare earth metals
chain.d: Baotou Steel Rare Earth (China — world's largest, state-backed). Neo Performance Materials (NMP — Silmet Estonia + Magnequench Zhenjiang — only significant Western REE metal production). Treibacher (Austria). Metal production is the second chokepoint after separation — you need both to manufacture magnets outside China.

chain.r: Sintered NdFeB: ~2kg/EV motor; ~600kg/MW direct-drive offshore wind
chain.d: Hitachi Metals/Proterial (Japan); TDK (Japan); Shin-Etsu Chemical (Japan); MP Materials (Fort Worth TX — first US mine-to-magnet since Molycorp 2015; OEM agreements with GM). >90% of global sintered NdFeB made in China. Japan + MP Materials = only credible non-Chinese NdFeB sources at meaningful scale.

chain.r: Motors, actuators (REE magnets); catalysts, phosphors (REE oxides)
chain.d: Nidec (Japan — world's largest motor company; EV motor + industrial motor exposure); Bosch; Denso. Robot motors: Fanuc, KUKA, ABB, Yaskawa — each industrial robot uses 6-12 REE permanent magnet motors totaling 2-3kg NdFeB. This is the robotics demand multiplier NOT in consensus REE forecasts.

chain.r: EV motors; direct-drive wind turbines; industrial robots; defense systems
chain.d: EV: Tesla, BYD, GM, Hyundai/Kia. Wind: Siemens Gamesa (SG 14-222, ~1,000kg NdFeB/unit). Robots: Fanuc, KUKA, ABB, Yaskawa — robotics buildout (AI-enabled automation + reshoring) is the underpriced REE demand multiplier. Defense: Raytheon, Northrop, L3Harris.

chain.r: Recovering NdPr + Dy/Tb from EV motors, wind turbines, hard drives
chain.d: Cyclic Materials (Canada, private — $53M Series A; DoD engagement; mine-to-magnet recycling approach); Urban Mining Co (US, private); REEtec (Norway — low-acid separation process); HyProMag (UK). Pre-commercial at scale but strongly DoD + EU Critical Raw Materials Act supported.

tm.note: Silver, Tin, Gallium, Germanium, Antimony, Cobalt. Small markets, extreme geographic concentration, high strategic value. China activated export controls on Ga/Ge (August 2023) and Sb (September 2024) — weaponization has begun. Tin is the most direct AI infrastructure metal: 3D chip packaging INCREASES tin intensity per unit of compute.

chain.r: Silver (Cu/Pb/Zn byproduct); Tin (cassiterite); Ga/Ge (Al/Zn byproduct); Co (DRC)
chain.d: Silver (~72% byproduct): Fresnillo (FRES.L — world's largest primary silver mine); First Majestic (AG); Pan American Silver (PAAS); Coeur (CDE). Tin: Yunnan Tin China (~30%); PT Timah Indonesia; Minsur Peru; Alphamin Resources (AFMJF — Bisie mine DRC, ~4.5% Sn grade, world's highest-grade tin mine). Ga/Ge: almost entirely Chinese byproduct — essentially no Western primary production.

chain.r: Primary silver mine development; tin exploration; Co DRC alternatives
chain.d: Silver: Silvercrest Metals (SIL.V — Las Chispas, best-in-class economics); Endeavour Silver (EXK — Mexico + Chile). Tin: Alphamin expansion at Bisie; Metals X (MLX.AX — Renison, Tasmania). Antimony: Perpetua Resources (PPTA — Stibnite Idaho; gold + antimony; DoD off-take MOU 2022 — uniquely positioned dual-product play).

chain.r: Silver doré; tin concentrate smelting; cobalt hydroxide precipitation
chain.d: Tin smelting: Yunnan Tin China (dominant); PT Timah Indonesia; Malaysia Smelting Corp (MSC — Penang; only major non-China/Indonesia listed tin smelter). Cobalt: Glencore (Murrin Murrin + Nikkelverk Norway — dominant Western Co refiner); Umicore (Belgium — Co refining into battery-grade sulfate). Ga/Ge refining: essentially Chinese monopoly.

chain.r: Silver → .999 LGD bars; SAC solder alloys; Co metal; GaN/GaAs substrates
chain.d: Silver refining: Asahi Refining (largest NA refiner, SLC); PAMP Suisse; Perth Mint. SAC solder (Sn96.5/Ag3/Cu0.5 — global RoHS electronics solder standard): MacDermid Alpha, Senju Metal Japan, Indium Corp. GaAs/GaN substrates: AXT Inc (AXTI — US company; key for power electronics, RF chips, and defense applications).

chain.r: Silver paste (solar cells); SAC solder paste; GaN power chips; NMC cobalt cathode
chain.d: Silver paste (solar): Heraeus Germany + DuPont (DD) — ~15-20mg Ag per cell, ~100T Ag/GW solar. SAC solder paste: MacDermid Alpha, Henkel. GaN power semiconductors (EV inverters, 5G, satellite): Wolfspeed (WOLF), Coherent (COHR), STM. NMC cobalt cathode: Umicore, BASF, Huayou.

chain.r: SMT PCB soldering; advanced AI chip packaging (CoWoS/HBM 3D stacking)
chain.d: Every PCB globally uses tin solder. Advanced AI packaging: TSMC CoWoS, ASE Group, Amkor (AMKR) — 3D chiplet + HBM stacking INCREASES solder joints per package. HBM uses micro-bump solder at <50 micron pitch. More AI compute = more tin per chip. This is the structural tin demand thesis.

chain.r: Tin from solder dross; Ag from industrial scrap; Co from batteries; Ge from fiber
chain.d: Tin: solder dross recycling — Aurubis, Metallo (Belgium). Silver: Asahi + PAMP handle large volumes of secondary industrial silver. Cobalt: Umicore (autocatalyst + battery recycling). Germanium: reclaimed from fiber optic cable scrap at Umicore + Teck Resources (Trail smelter, British Columbia).

pg.note: South Africa (75% of Pt, ~40% Rh) + Russia (40% of Pd via Norilsk — largely unavailable post-sanctions). Palladium in structural ICE-to-EV demand decline. Platinum has hydrogen economy demand story (PEM electrolyzers) — a 2028-2035 wave. Gold is the monetary/geopolitical hedge. Central bank buying at record levels since 2022.

chain.r: Streaming on PGM mines; physical ETF exposure; gold royalties
chain.d: WPM (Stillwater Pd+Au stream — only significant Western Pd stream from a non-Russian source). Physical ETFs: PPLT, PALL, SPPP. Gold streamers: FNV, WPM, RGLD, SAND — gold streaming is the most liquid and developed royalty/streaming market globally. Physical gold: GLD (~900T), IAU.

chain.r: PGM reef exploration in Bushveld Complex (SA) and Stillwater (Montana)
chain.d: Stillwater Complex (Montana) — only significant US/Western PGM deposit. SBSW owns both Stillwater + East Boulder mines. Platinum Group Metals (PTM.TO — Waterberg project SA; one of last large undeveloped Bushveld deposits — palladium-rich).

chain.r: Deep-level underground (SA Bushveld); open-pit (Mogalakwena); Stillwater (Montana)
chain.d: Sibanye Stillwater (SBSW — SA gold + US PGM + SA PGM; persistent Eskom power + labor challenges); Anglo American Platinum (AMS.J — Mogalakwena open pit, world's largest Pt mine; Amandelbult); Impala (IMP.J — Rustenburg + Zimplats Zimbabwe); Northam. Norilsk Nickel (GMKN — 40% global Pd; NOT investable post-2022 sanctions).

chain.r: Silver as primary product from high-grade vein deposits in Mexico + Americas
chain.d: First Majestic (AG — highest Ag price leverage but also highest cost); Pan American Silver (PAAS — diversified Americas portfolio); Coeur Mining (CDE — Rochester NV + Palmarejo Mexico); Silvercrest Metals (SIL.V — Las Chispas, best-in-class economics in primary silver mining).

chain.r: Converting PGM ore through smelting → PGM-rich matte; multi-step refining
chain.d: Anglo American Platinum (Polokwane smelter + PMR Rustenburg); Implats (Rustenburg refinery); SBSW smelting operations. Norilsk Kola refinery (Russia) processes ~40% of global Pd — largely isolated from Western buyers post-2022 sanctions.

chain.r: Autocatalysts (Pt/Pd/Rh); hydrogen fuel cells (Pt); jewelry; industrial catalysts
chain.d: Autocatalysts: ~80% of Pt/Pd/Rh demand currently. JM (world leader in PGM chemistry + autocatalyst design); BASF; Umicore. Hydrogen PEM electrolyzers: ITM Power (ITM.L), Nel ASA (NEL.OL). Fuel cells: BLDP, BE. Gold: central bank buying at record levels 2022-2024.

chain.r: Investment bars/coins/ETFs; central bank reserve assets; electronics wire bonding
chain.d: Gold is a monetary + geopolitical hedge — driven by real rates, USD strength, and central bank reserve diversification. Net central bank purchases at record levels 2022-2024: China, India, Turkey, Poland, Singapore explicitly diversifying away from US Treasuries. Electronics: Au bonding wire in semiconductor packages (TSMC, Intel, Samsung).

chain.r: Recovering Pt/Pd/Rh from spent vehicle catalysts — ~30-40% of Pt/Pd supply
chain.d: JM (world leader — both primary PGM chemistry + autocatalyst recycling globally); Umicore (Hoboken Belgium — world's most advanced precious metals recycling operation); SBSW recycling division. ~30% Pt and ~40% Pd demand now met from recycled autocatalysts — grows as ICE fleet ages.

wl.note: Cross-cutting production input — not a utility play. Water limits Atacama copper/lithium output, constrains nuclear SMR siting, and is being rationed for AI data centers in Arizona. Land adjacent to infrastructure corridors is an emerging institutional asset class.

chain.r: Legal ownership in scarce basins; senior rights compound as demand grows
chain.d: Vidler Water Resources (VWTR — Nevada/AZ/NM water rights; Pacific Enterprises aquifer; rare listed pure-play); Consolidated Water (CWCO). Most Western US water rights privately held. Atacama water rights in Chile: SQM/Codelco disputes with indigenous communities directly constrain copper + lithium output.

chain.r: Surface/groundwater delivery to industrial off-takers under contract or utility tariffs
chain.d: AWK (largest US water utility); WTRG (water + gas distribution); YORW; SJW Group. Data center water demand = newest growth driver — Microsoft, Amazon, Google becoming among the largest industrial water users in Arizona, Virginia, and North Carolina.

chain.r: Treating raw water to process specification for mining, power, data centers
chain.d: Veolia Water Technologies (France); Xylem (XYL — dominant US industrial water platform post-Evoqua merger; also Sensus smart metering); AECOM + Jacobs (J) for large water EPC projects. Mine process water treatment (acid mine drainage — AMD) is a growing specialized segment.

chain.r: Freshwater from seawater/brackish groundwater; prerequisite for Atacama expansion
chain.d: Energy Recovery (ERII — ~50% global market share in RO pressure exchange devices; near-monopoly niche; expanding into industrial + US municipal + defense/naval). IDE Technologies (private); Acciona Agua; BWT AG. BHP Escondida + Codelco operate large seawater desal plants at 3,200m altitude in the Atacama — prerequisite for any copper/lithium production expansion.

chain.r: Integrated water supply, tailings water recovery, pit dewatering
chain.d: Weir Group (WEIR.L — slurry pumps + hydrocyclones); Metso (METSO.HE — filtration presses for dry tailings — the post-Brumadinho dam collapse imperative globally); Xylem (dewatering pumps); Nalco Water (Ecolab subsidiary — mining process chemistry).

chain.r: Managing 3-10 barrels water per barrel of oil in shale plays
chain.d: ARIS Water Solutions (ARIS — Permian Basin focus; largest dedicated Permian water midstream company); Select Water Solutions (WTTR — broader basin coverage). Permian produced water is a >$10B/yr service market.

chain.r: Industrial-scale cooling water for hyperscale DCs and thermal power plants
chain.d: Vertiv (VRT — DC cooling; liquid direct-to-chip cooling fastest-growing as GPU cluster power density exceeds 100kW per rack); Modine Manufacturing (MOD — DC thermal management). Arizona regulators denying new DC water permits in Phoenix metro — water availability is now a binding AI compute site selection constraint.

chain.r: Right-of-way, substation sites, LNG terminal land, data center parcels
chain.d: Emerging institutional asset class: BIP/BIPC (Brookfield), Macquarie, Blackstone assembling land adjacent to US power transmission corridors and DC development corridors. Not yet a clean listed pure-play. Analogous to the Crown Castle/SBA Communications land-under-towers model applied to power infrastructure.

chain.r: Smart metering, membrane tech, aquifer mapping, remote sensing
chain.d: Xylem (XYL — Sensus smart metering + analytics + Evoqua process water); Itron (ITRI — smart water meters); Roper Technologies (ROP — Neptune meters + flow measurement); Pentair (PNR — filtration); Planet Labs (PL — remote sensing of water bodies and mine tailings); Trimble (TRMB).

ii.note: Industrial gases (Linde/APD — Western duopoly; utility-like pricing power; near-infinite customer switching costs via on-site plant model), specialty metals (Ti, Be), and strategic minerals (He, boron). Materion (MTRN) is the US beryllium near-monopoly — underowned, defense-critical, <$1.5B market cap.

chain.r: He-bearing gas field rights; mineral sands (Ti, REE); boron deposits
chain.d: Helium: Hugoton field (US, federal BLM reserve), Qatar North Field (~35% global He), Amur GPP Russia (stranded by sanctions — tightening global supply). Rio Tinto Boron (CA — 20 Mule Team Borax brand) + Eti Maden (Turkey, state) control global borate market. Mineral sands (Ti feedstock): Iluka (ILU.AX), Tronox (TROX).

chain.r: He field delineation; ASU on-site plant project development
chain.d: Helium One Global (HELO — Tanzania He, pre-production). Most investable He exposure is indirect via LIN/APD. ASU on-site plant development: Linde + APD build dedicated plants at large customers under 15-20yr sole-source contracts — each plant is a $50-200M capital investment with near-infinite switching cost.

chain.r: ASUs produce O2/N2/Ar from air; He extracted from natural gas streams
chain.d: Linde (LIN — world's largest industrial gas company; >20% global market share; sole-source on-site plants at TSMC, Samsung, Intel globally); Air Products (APD — second-largest; major MENA/Asia infrastructure projects); Air Liquide (AI.PA — third, strong Europe/Asia). Once an on-site plant is built for a customer, switching cost is effectively infinite.

chain.r: Ti sponge (Kroll process); boron chemicals; beryllium mining + processing
chain.d: ATI Inc (ATI — largest US specialty metals; Ti + Ni + Co + Cr alloys; primary beneficiary of Boeing/Airbus diversification away from VSMPO-AVISMA Russia post-2022); Howmet Aerospace (HWM — precision cast turbine blades for GE Aerospace + RTX). Materion Corp (MTRN — ONLY Western beryllium mine at Spor Mountain UT + ONLY US Be processing; near-monopoly in defense-critical niche; <$1.5B market cap; systematically underowned).

chain.r: UHP industrial gases for semiconductor/pharma; cylinder/dewar filling
chain.d: Linde, APD, Air Liquide produce ultra-high purity grades for semiconductor fab and pharmaceutical applications. Semiconductor-grade process gases (UHP N2O, SiH4, HCl, NF3) are high-margin. Matheson (private, Nippon Sanso subsidiary) + Airgas (Air Liquide subsidiary) for specialty cylinder gas distribution.

chain.r: Bulk liquid delivery via cryogenic tanker trucks; cylinder distribution
chain.d: Chart Industries (GTLS — broadest cryogenic equipment portfolio globally: storage tanks, trailers, heat exchangers, vaporizers; also LNG + hydrogen equipment — unique multi-thematic position). Cryostar (private, Linde subsidiary). Graham Corp (GHM — defense/aerospace cryogenic, small-cap).

chain.r: He for NMR/MRI; O2/N2 for bioreactors; Ti for implants; UHP gases for fab chambers
chain.d: Pharma NMR/MRI: every hospital + lab depends on liquid He for superconducting magnets. Semiconductor fabs: LIN + APD are sole-source 20yr suppliers to TSMC, Samsung, Intel. Ti medical: ATI, Zimmer Biomet (ZBH), Stryker (SYK). Ti aerospace: ATI + HWM for jet engine turbine blades (GE Aerospace, RTX). Defense Be: Materion (MTRN) for nuclear warheads + defense electronics.

chain.r: Cryogenic plant EPC; gas handling equipment; membrane separation
chain.d: Chart Industries (GTLS — broadest cryogenic equipment portfolio across gas, LNG, and hydrogen; Howden compressor + fan division adds industrial crossover). Graham Corp (GHM — defense/aerospace cryogenic). Ametek (AME — gas analyzers). Pentair (PNR — filtration crossover with water).

fert.note: Food security was weaponized in 2022 — Russia/Belarus used fertilizer export restrictions as deliberate foreign policy tools. Canada (Nutrien, Mosaic) became strategically critical as the allied-nation potash alternative. Morocco OCP controls ~70% of global phosphate reserves — a long-term chokepoint. Phosphate is non-renewable. Nutrien is the only complete NPK company globally.

chain.r: Potash evaporite deposits (Saskatchewan, Belarus, Russia, Jordan); phosphate rock
chain.d: Saskatchewan potash (NTR, MOS) = largest Western allied-nation resource base. Morocco OCP Group (state-owned) controls ~70% of global phosphate reserves — geopolitical chokepoint to monitor long-term. Russia/Belarus potash (Uralkali, Belaruskali) subject to Western sanctions but still reaches market via third-party intermediaries.

chain.r: New potash basin delineation; phosphate feasibility; ammonia plant siting
chain.d: BHP Jansen Stage 1 (Saskatchewan — world-scale new potash mine; production expected ~2026-2027). K+S Bethune mine (Canada). New nitrogen plants tied to natural gas price access. Saudi Arabia Maaden Phosphate (Mosaic JV) for phosphate development.

chain.r: Underground/solution mining for potash; open pit for phosphate rock
chain.d: Nutrien (NTR — world's largest K producer + significant N+P; ONLY complete NPK company globally; Canpotex JV with Mosaic controls all Canadian potash export logistics and pricing globally). Mosaic (MOS — Esterhazy K Saskatchewan + Florida P mining). ICL Group (Israel — Dead Sea Works K + Rotem P). OCP Morocco (state-owned — the phosphate chokepoint).

chain.r: Potash → MOP (60% K2O); phosphate rock → phosphoric acid; gas → ammonia
chain.d: Nitrogen (Haber-Bosch from natural gas + air — Henry Hub is ~80% of variable cost): CF Industries (CF — largest US N producer; enormous Henry Hub leverage); Yara International (YAR.OL — world's largest N producer); OCI NV (OCI.AS). Phosphate acid process: Mosaic (Florida), OCP (Morocco).

chain.r: NPK blends, urea granulation, ammonium nitrate; precision formulations
chain.d: Nutrien (NTR — ONLY complete NPK under one roof globally; cross-sells all three nutrients through Nutrien Ag Solutions retail network — unique scale + distribution moat). Mosaic (MOS — K+P specialist). OCP Group (Morocco — world's largest phosphate + fertilizer exporter). Yara (N specialist + NPK blends).

chain.r: Capesize/Panamax bulk vessels; rail and barge; port terminals; warehousing
chain.d: Nutrien owns port facilities (Vancouver BC, Portland OR, New Orleans LA). Canpotex (NTR + MOS JV) controls Canadian potash export logistics globally. ADM, Cargill, Louis Dreyfus handle global logistics for OCP and others.

chain.r: Benchmark pricing; contract vs. spot markets; seasonal demand patterns
chain.d: Potash historically priced via cartels: Canpotex (Canada) + BPC (Belarus/Russia — effectively collapsed 2022 when sanctions severed Belaruskali from Uralkali; potash pricing now more market-determined). CME Group (CME) provides listed fertilizer futures for urea, DAP, potash, and ammonia.

chain.r: Farmer application; precision agriculture services; crop protection
chain.d: End consumers are farmers globally — fertilizer demand is relatively price-inelastic for staple crops (corn, wheat, rice, soy). Precision agriculture: John Deere (DE — Operations Center platform, global standard); Trimble (TRMB — precision positioning + ag software); Corteva (CTVA — seeds + crop protection).

chain.r: Phosphorus recovery from wastewater; organic fertilizers; nutrient cycling
chain.d: Phosphate is non-renewable — unlike K (abundant salt deposits) and N (synthesized from air), P cannot be economically synthesized and reserves are finite. Phosphorus recovery from municipal wastewater via struvite precipitation: Ostara Nutrient Recovery Technologies (private, Canada); NuReSys (Belgium). Growing strategically under circular economy policy.
